blocks.content: He took her on a Saturday in November when the sky was the flat white of late autumn and the fields had gone brown and still.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's just a view. Just a thing I said I would do.
The water tower hadn't changed. He'd been up here four times in his life — three of them with Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: Okay. This is worth the climb.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: You can see the whole town.
He pointed it out. She leaned over the railing to look, and he stood in the same spot he'd stood before — same wind, same view, same field below.
He didn't think about that either.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: I get why you like it up here.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: Is that the field? Behind those houses?
She turned to look at him instead of the view, and the catwalk was narrow and she was close, and he already knew what was coming and he didn't move.
